title: Keystone Consumer Config
description: Top-level keystone.json at the consumer repo root. Records the harness folder name, the framework version pin, and the nested plugin tree. The project layer (harness root contents) wins by default; among plugins, outer (shallower in the tree) wins over plugins nested inside; a plugin's `strict` declarations lock items absolutely. See PLAN-1.0.md §3 for full semantics.

version.description: Schema version of keystone.json itself. Currently "1".

framework_version.description: Keystone binary version this project is pinned to (SemVer).

harness_root.description: Directory name where the harness is installed inside the repo. Defaults to "harness" when omitted. Downstream commands read this so users do not have to re-pass --harness-root after init.

plugins.description: Ordered list of plugin nodes. Among plugins, outer plugins (shallower in this tree) win over plugins nested inside them. The project layer always wins by default; a plugin's `strict` declarations are the only thing that locks an item against the project.

budgets.description: Optional per-port context-budget caps. Phase 5 wires these into keystone doctor --budget.

name.description: Plugin name. Must match the manifest's name. Lowercase, kebab-case, ≤64 chars.

source.description: Shorthand source: `[<host>/]<owner>/<repo>`. When the host is omitted (single slash, no dots in the first segment), the resolver prepends `github.com`. Examples: `tacoda/tacoda-org`, `github.com/acme/policies`, `gitlab.com/acme/team/policies`.

version.description: Git ref (tag, branch, or SHA). Required.

strict.description: Per-port lock list. A strict item is absolute — nothing else (project, sibling plugins, descendant plugins) can override it. Key is the port name (e.g. "guides", "sensors"); value is a list of item basenames (no .md extension).

children.description: Child plugins nested under this one. Among plugins, this parent wins over its children for non-strict items.
